# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController    # define your controller class here  
     def create     
          @image = Image.new(img_params)       // Assuming you have a form with file input and validations for images, these can be moved to 'create' action later on in the code 
         if params[:format] && params[:size].present?    # If format or size is present       
            command = "convert '#{@image.current_path}' -resize \"#{params[:size]}x#{params[:size]}\" 'outputfile.jpg'"   // This line will depend on your specific ImageMagick configuration and requirements 
           system(command) or raise("#{'ImageMagick conversion failed.'}") if @image.errors || params!(:format, :current_path).present?    # If something is wrong with the image (like file type not being jpeg for example), it will rescue here     
         end 
          redirect_to yourdefinedredirecthere     // Redirection depends on what you need to do after conversion. You may want another page, a notification or nothing at all depending on requirement   # Include logic where if there was no error the user is send back image otherwise show an appropriate message in views etc 
      end    else render :new           // Error handling for incorrect inputs can be added here as per your application's requirements. For now it will just re-render form again with errors present unless you have a valid format/size pair on post request  
     rescue Exception => e        # General exception handler to catch any other exceptions 
      render :error, status: :internal_server_error       // Renders an error page when something unexpected happens i.e no route or action is hit which has not been defined in controller actions    and sets http response as internal server error (500) if anything goes wrong  
     end 
      private def img_params         # Assuming you have a form with file input, the params are being passed to this method. You might need additional validation/processing for images depending on your application's requirements   
       params.require(:image).permit(:current_path)   // This is assuming that `:imagable` action in controller accepts current image and only :file will be included if you want the form to handle file uploading, not just conversion of uploaded files 
     end            # Assuming there's a table named 'images', it should have columns for storing paths (current_path) & error status. Depending on your db schema also define appropriate validations   and permissions accordingly    if @image && (!@imgble || (@user != current user)) then render :accessdenied end 
end     # This controller does not handle image conversions to different formats unless the format or size is provided in post request. You can extend this code with other actions according your requirements, for example: action method like `convert_to` which will convert uploaded file into specifics format based on user-specified parameters
